text.title: Graph-Aware Tools: The Narrow Exception
text.summary: Type checkers and graph-aware tools (`tsc -p`, `mypy --strict`, `cargo check`) need the project graph; scope them per-project, never per-file, and never whole-monorepo at once.

section.title: Per-project, not per-file, not whole-tree
section.p: Some tools cannot answer "is file X correct?" without the rest of the project — types resolve through imports. Per-file scoping breaks them; whole-monorepo scoping kills the hook latency budget. Per-project scoping splits the difference.

rule.statement: For graph-aware tools (`tsc`, `mypy`, `cargo check`, `pyright`), the pre-commit hook MUST run per-project (one `tsconfig.json`, one `pyproject.toml`, one `Cargo.toml`) and MUST set `pass_filenames: false` so the framework does not pass per-file args. The hook MUST be triggered ONLY when files inside that project change (use `pre-commit`'s `files:` regex or `lefthook`'s `glob:`). Whole-monorepo invocations of these tools belong in CI, not in pre-commit.
rule.rationale: Calling `tsc index.ts` resolves only that file and reports false negatives because cross-file type contracts are missed. Calling `tsc -p tsconfig.json` from a hook that fires on every commit is correct but slow on large projects; gating on the project's own file glob makes the hook a no-op when the user only edited a sibling project. The pre-commit framework's `files:` regex and `pass_filenames: false` flag exist exactly for this case.

example.code: # .pre-commit-config.yaml - repo: local hooks: - id: tsc-api name: tsc (api project) entry: bash -c "cd packages/api && npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json" language: system files: ^packages/api/.*\.tsx?$ pass_filenames: false
example.note: Hook runs only when files under `packages/api/` change; it ignores the file list and uses the project's tsconfig instead.

example.code: - repo: local hooks: - id: tsc entry: npx tsc --noEmit language: system files: \.tsx?$ pass_filenames: true # THE BUG: tsc gets a file list, ignores tsconfig project graph
example.note: tsc invoked with bare filenames produces unreliable output — the type graph isn't loaded.

antipattern.description: Wiring a single `tsc` hook for an entire monorepo, scoped to all `*.ts` files.
antipattern.reason: Every commit anywhere in the monorepo triggers a full type-check of every project. Latency goes to minutes, the floor collapses, agents bypass.
